evidence_quotes.quote: Extensive regulation on in-game purchases in order to severely curtail in-game gambling
The obligation to provide end-of-life features to ensure a game purchased remains playable in some state.

paragraphs.text: Hello, thank you for hearing me out. I'd like the Digital Fairness Act to include a few things: 1) Extensive regulation on in-game purchases in order to severely curtail in-game gambling, which right now happens in games for children, like any recent FIFA games, or many mobile games (like Nintendo's Mario Kart or Niantic's Pokemon Go). I am fine with games making a profit, I am less fine with games profiting from children and disabled people through addictive gambling. 2) The obligation to provide end-of-life features to ensure a game purchased remains playable in some state. Not asking for endless servers or unlimited support, just for a game, any game bought, to be playable in some way. This is what the Stop Killing Games initiative (which has garnered 1.4 million signatures within the EU) is currently supporting. 3) Some worker protections for developers. Layoffs have been extensive in the video game industry lately despite the industry itself remaining extremely profitable, and some protection for workers seem in order here. Workers have been subjected to what has been generously called "crunch", having to work for 70 to 100 hours per week for extensive periods of time. This is abuse and should stop. There also have been cases of systemic sexual harassment (referring here specifically to Ubisoft and Activision-Blizzard, the former being a European-based company), which could be stopped through stricter oversight. Thank you for your attention,
